_note: Demo seed for Sumnohow CFO demo. All date values use TODAY+N placeholders — replaced at reset time so exposures are always future-dated. Update rates annually if market moves significantly.
_story: 7 exposures, ~EUR 40M+ portfolio. GBP/USD + CHF/USD: onside hedges, positive P&L. EUR/USD: BREACH — hedges placed at wrong rate. EUR/NOK: OPEN — fires recommendation. GBP/NOK: HEDGED, strong P&L. JPY/USD: 100% hedged. USD/NOK: offside hedge, margin call risk.
_placeholders: TODAY+N is replaced with isoformat date N days from reset date. Supported: 30,60,75,90,105,120,135,150,165,180,195,210,225,240,270.
_facility_note: facility_limits: slot 0 = first active facility in DB. Reset sets limit_eur to show ~70% utilisation. Slot 0 total tranche notional ≈ EUR 7M (GBP 1.5M + GBP 1.8M + JPY 500M ≈ 6.9M EUR) → 10M limit ≈ 69%.

exposures.description: UK commodity procurement — Q2/Q3 delivery, Liverpool port
exposures._story: 50% hedged — IN PROGRESS. Two tranches both below budget rate = positive locked P&L.

tranches.notes: DNB Markets — locked before GBP strengthened. Ref DNB-GB-240301-01.
DNB Markets — topped up, best rate of the program. Ref DNB-GB-240318-02.

exposures.description: European supplier invoices — H1 2026, Amsterdam and Hamburg
exposures._story: 65% hedged — BREACH. Hedges placed when EUR was weaker; current spot has moved above both the hedge rates and budget rate. MTM loss triggers BREACH status.

tranches.notes: Nordea Treasury — EUR/USD fwd, hedged when EUR briefly dipped. Ref NOR-EU-240210-01.
Nordea Treasury — second tranche, rate slightly worse. Ref NOR-EU-240225-02.

exposures.description: EUR-denominated supplier invoices — Oslo HQ operational costs
exposures._story: 0% hedged — OPEN. Recommendation engine will flag this prominently.

exposures.description: UK export receivables — North Sea contracts, multiple shipments
exposures._story: 85% hedged — HEDGED. Three tranches, all above budget rate = strong positive locked P&L.

tranches.notes: SEB Corporate Banking — GBP/NOK first tranche, above budget. Ref SEB-GBN-240115-01.
SEB Corporate Banking — second tranche, rate above budget. Ref SEB-GBN-240205-02.
DNB Markets — opportunistic hedge on GBP strength. Best rate in program. Ref DNB-GBN-240310-03.

exposures.description: Swiss precision equipment imports — single shipment Q3
exposures._story: 50% hedged — IN PROGRESS. One executed tranche below budget = positive locked P&L.

tranches.notes: Nordea Treasury — CHF/USD forward, hedged below budget. Ref NOR-CHF-240320-01.

exposures.description: Japanese manufacturing components — rolling quarterly, Yokohama port
exposures._story: 100% hedged — HEDGED. Two tranches fully covering the exposure. JPY/USD below budget = positive P&L on large notional.

tranches.notes: DNB Markets — JPY/USD first tranche, below budget. Ref DNB-JPY-240218-01.
DNB Markets — second tranche, even better rate. Fully covered. Ref DNB-JPY-240305-02.

exposures.description: USD-denominated import payments — North American grain, Houston port
exposures._story: 30% hedged — IN PROGRESS. CRITICAL: hedge rate (11.12) above current USD/NOK spot (~10.80) = offside ~3%. MTM loss triggers the single margin call risk flag in the demo.

tranches.notes: SEB Corporate Banking — USD/NOK forward, hedged when USD was stronger. Now offside vs spot. Ref SEB-USD-240228-01.
